title: TRANQUIL â€“ Privacy Dashboard, Data Ownership Controls & Secure Deletion

requirements.text: Implement a Privacy Dashboard page (route: /privacy) accessible from the Settings page and the Profile page. The dashboard must display: a stored data summary (counts of stress logs, mood entries, sleep entries, breathing sessions, device pairing records); a plain-language data usage explanation section; toggles for analytics tracking and AI prediction usage (persisted to the backend user profile); a 'Manage Connected Devices' section listing paired devices with individual remove buttons; and a prominently styled red 'Delete Account' button at the bottom. The page must follow the TRANQUIL design system (OKLCH palette, Nunito/Quicksand fonts, dark/light theme support) and be fully responsive from 320 px mobile through desktop.

quotes: Create a dedicated Privacy & Data Control section with: View stored data summary, Data usage explanation, Toggle analytics tracking, Toggle AI prediction usage, Manage connected devices, Delete account button (highlighted in red)

acceptanceCriteria: A /privacy route is accessible from Settings and Profile pages.
Data summary cards show real counts fetched from the backend for stress logs, mood entries, sleep entries, breathing sessions, and paired devices.
Analytics tracking toggle and AI prediction toggle are rendered as Switch components and their state is persisted to the backend user profile.
Connected devices section lists stored pairing records; each entry has a 'Remove Device' button that deletes that record from the backend.
Delete Account button is styled with a red background and the label 'Delete My Account'.
Page is fully responsive at 320 px, 768 px, and 1024 px+ viewports.
Dark and light themes render correctly with no visual discrepancies.

requirements.text: Implement a full account deletion flow. In the Privacy Dashboard, when the user clicks 'Delete My Account', display a modal warning with the exact text 'This action is permanent and cannot be undone.' and a secondary message explaining that all data (profile, stress logs, mood journal, sleep data, device pairing history) will be deleted. Require the user to type 'DELETE' to confirm. On confirmation, call a backend function that marks the account for deletion (soft-delete flag with a 30-day grace period timestamp). Immediately clear all locally cached data and log the user out. Display a toast confirming deletion initiation. The entire UI flow must complete within 30 seconds. Add a corresponding backend Motoko function `requestAccountDeletion(userId)` that sets a `pendingDeletion` flag and `deletionScheduledAt` timestamp on the user record.

quotes: Allows users to permanently delete their account from Settings.
Provides a 7-day recovery grace period (optional soft delete).
Clear warning message: 'This action is permanent and cannot be undone.'
Data deletion should complete within 30 seconds

acceptanceCriteria: Clicking 'Delete My Account' opens a confirmation modal with the exact warning text.
The confirm button remains disabled until the user types 'DELETE' in the input field.
On confirmation, the backend `requestAccountDeletion` function is called and returns success.
The backend marks the user record with `pendingDeletion = true` and records `deletionScheduledAt` timestamp.
After confirmation, all React Query caches are cleared and the user is logged out via Internet Identity.
A success toast 'Your account deletion has been scheduled. You have 30 days to cancel.' is shown before logout.
The entire flow from button click to logout completes within 30 seconds.

requirements.text: Implement selective data deletion controls. In the Privacy Dashboard, add a 'Manage My Data' section with the following individual deletion actions: (1) Delete a specific stress log entry â€” each stress log in the existing list views must have a delete icon that calls the backend `deleteStressReading(id)` function; (2) Delete a specific mood journal entry â€” each mood entry in the Journal page must have a delete icon that calls the backend `deleteMoodEntry(id)` function; (3) Clear all analytics history (weekly/monthly) â€” a 'Clear Analytics History' button that calls a backend function `clearAnalyticsHistory()` deleting all stress readings for the user; (4) Reset device pairing data without deleting the account â€” a 'Reset Device Data' button that calls `clearDevicePairings()` on the backend. All destructive actions must show a brief inline confirmation ('Are you sure?') before executing. Backend must implement or expose these deletion functions in the main Motoko actor.

quotes: Reset only device data without deleting account.

acceptanceCriteria: Each stress log entry in the Analytics page and any log list renders a delete icon button.
Clicking a stress log delete icon shows an inline 'Are you sure?' prompt; confirming calls the backend and removes the entry from the UI.
Each mood journal entry in the Journal page renders a delete icon button with the same inline confirmation pattern.
Privacy Dashboard 'Clear Analytics History' button triggers a backend call that removes all stress readings for the user; UI reflects the cleared state.
Privacy Dashboard 'Reset Device Data' button triggers a backend call that removes all device pairing records; the Manage Connected Devices list becomes empty.
All four backend functions (`deleteStressReading`, `deleteMoodEntry`, `clearAnalyticsHistory`, `clearDevicePairings`) exist in `backend/main.mo` with proper authorization checks.

requirements.text: Enhance the existing data export feature in Settings to support three export formats: JSON (already partially implemented in `frontend/src/utils/dataExport.ts`), CSV, and a structured PDF summary report. Add a format selector (radio group or segmented control: JSON / CSV / PDF) before the download button. The JSON export must include all user data (profile, stress readings, mood entries, sleep entries, breathing sessions). The CSV export must produce a flat spreadsheet of stress readings with columns: date, stressScore, heartRate, skinTemp, motion. The PDF export must generate a formatted summary report with the user's name, avatar, total sessions, average stress score, mood trend, and a 7-day stress chart as text-based ASCII or table. All exports must be triggered as in-app browser downloads. Extend `frontend/src/utils/dataExport.ts` with `exportAsCSV` and `exportAsPDF` utility functions.

quotes: Add an option: 'Download My Data'. Export as: JSON, CSV, PDF summary report. Delivered securely via email or in-app download.

acceptanceCriteria: Settings page export section shows a format selector with JSON, CSV, and PDF options.
Selecting JSON and clicking download triggers the existing JSON export, producing a timestamped `.json` file.
Selecting CSV and clicking download produces a `.csv` file with columns: date, stressScore, heartRate, skinTemp, motion.

Selecting PDF and clicking download produces a `.pdf` file (or a `.html` print-ready file) with a structured summary report including user name, total sessions, average stress score, and a readable 7-day data table.
`frontend/src/utils/dataExport.ts` exports `exportAsCSV` and `exportAsPDF` functions.
All downloads use a timestamped filename pattern (e.g., `tranquil-export-2024-01-15.csv`).

requirements.text: Add backend Motoko data model and storage support for: (1) a `pendingDeletion: Bool` and `deletionScheduledAt: ?Int` field on the `UserProfile` type; (2) a `devicePairings: [DevicePairing]` array on the user profile storing `{ deviceId: Text; deviceName: Text; pairedAt: Int }`; (3) privacy preference fields on `UserProfile`: `analyticsEnabled: Bool` and `aiPredictionEnabled: Bool`. Expose the following new actor functions with caller-authorization: `requestAccountDeletion()`, `cancelAccountDeletion()`, `deleteStressReading(id: Nat)`, `deleteMoodEntry(id: Nat)`, `clearAnalyticsHistory()`, `addDevicePairing(deviceId: Text, deviceName: Text)`, `removeDevicePairing(deviceId: Text)`, `clearDevicePairings()`, `updatePrivacyPreferences(analyticsEnabled: Bool, aiPredictionEnabled: Bool)`. All mutation functions must verify `msg.caller` matches the record owner before modifying data.

quotes: the app must give users full ownership control over their mental health data
Backend Deletion Flow: Re-authenticate user, Delete related collections, Delete authentication record, Invalidate active tokens, Confirm deletion to user, Log deletion event (without personal data)

acceptanceCriteria: `UserProfile` type in `backend/main.mo` includes `pendingDeletion`, `deletionScheduledAt`, `analyticsEnabled`, `aiPredictionEnabled`, and `devicePairings` fields.
`requestAccountDeletion()` sets `pendingDeletion = true` and `deletionScheduledAt` to current time and returns `#ok`.
`cancelAccountDeletion()` clears the pending deletion flag and returns `#ok`.
`deleteStressReading(id)` removes the specified reading only if it belongs to `msg.caller`, returns `#err(#Unauthorized)` otherwise.
`deleteMoodEntry(id)` removes the specified entry only if it belongs to `msg.caller`.
`clearAnalyticsHistory()` removes all stress readings for `msg.caller`.
`addDevicePairing`, `removeDevicePairing`, `clearDevicePairings` manage the `devicePairings` array scoped to `msg.caller`.
`updatePrivacyPreferences` updates `analyticsEnabled` and `aiPredictionEnabled` for `msg.caller`.
All functions return `Result<(), AppError>` and include `#Unauthorized` error variant checks.

requirements.text: Add a GDPR/compliance UI layer: (1) On first login (after Internet Identity auth, before profile setup), display a Terms & Conditions and Privacy Policy acceptance modal. The modal must show a scrollable summary of data practices, a checkbox 'I accept the Terms & Conditions and Privacy Policy', and a 'Continue' button that is disabled until the checkbox is checked. Record `termsAcceptedAt: ?Int` on the user profile backend. (2) Add a 'Privacy Policy' and 'Terms & Conditions' link in the Settings page footer that opens a modal with the full policy text. (3) In the Privacy Dashboard, add a 'Data Usage Explanation' section describing in plain language what data is collected, why it is used, and how long it is retained.

quotes: Compliance Requirements: GDPR principles, Indian IT Data Protection standards, Clear Privacy Policy integration, Terms & Conditions acceptance tracking.

acceptanceCriteria: First-time users see a T&C / Privacy Policy acceptance modal before the profile setup modal.
The 'Continue' button on the acceptance modal is disabled until the acceptance checkbox is checked.
Accepting the T&C calls a backend function that records `termsAcceptedAt` timestamp on the user profile.
Users who have already accepted are not shown the modal again on subsequent logins.
Settings page footer contains 'Privacy Policy' and 'Terms & Conditions' links that open a read-only modal.
Privacy Dashboard includes a 'Data Usage Explanation' section with plain-language descriptions of data collection, purpose, and retention period.
`UserProfile` in `backend/main.mo` includes a `termsAcceptedAt: ?Int` field and a `recordTermsAcceptance()` function.

requirements.text: Update the React Query hooks in `frontend/src/hooks/useQueries.ts` to include mutations and queries for all new backend functions: `useRequestAccountDeletion`, `useCancelAccountDeletion`, `useDeleteStressReading`, `useDeleteMoodEntry`, `useClearAnalyticsHistory`, `useAddDevicePairing`, `useRemoveDevicePairing`, `useClearDevicePairings`, `useUpdatePrivacyPreferences`, `useRecordTermsAcceptance`. Each mutation must invalidate the relevant query cache keys on success. Add a `useGetUserPrivacyPreferences` query that reads `analyticsEnabled`, `aiPredictionEnabled`, `devicePairings`, `pendingDeletion`, and `termsAcceptedAt` from the user profile.

quotes: the app must give users full ownership control over their mental health data, ensuring transparency, security, and trust

acceptanceCriteria: All listed mutation hooks exist and are exported from `useQueries.ts`.
Each mutation hook calls the corresponding backend actor method.
On success, each mutation invalidates the appropriate React Query cache keys (e.g., `['userProfile']`, `['stressReadings']`, `['moodEntries']`).
`useGetUserPrivacyPreferences` returns `analyticsEnabled`, `aiPredictionEnabled`, `devicePairings`, `pendingDeletion`, and `termsAcceptedAt` from the user profile query.
TypeScript types are correctly inferred with no `any` casts.

requirements.text: Apply a cohesive visual upgrade to the TRANQUIL app to make it more engaging and premium. Introduce a calm, therapeutic visual identity: deep teal-to-indigo gradient backgrounds on the Dashboard hero area, soft glassmorphism card surfaces (frosted glass effect with backdrop-blur), subtle ambient glow effects on the stress gauge and breathing circle, smooth entrance animations (fade-in + slide-up) on page load, and micro-interaction hover/press states on all interactive elements. Update the color palette in `frontend/src/index.css` and `frontend/tailwind.config.js` to introduce richer jewel-tone accent colors (teal, sage green, soft gold) while preserving the existing OKLCH variable structure. All changes must remain consistent across dark and light themes and all breakpoints.

quotes: develop the app in more interesting and more opportunities way

acceptanceCriteria: Dashboard hero section displays a teal-to-indigo gradient background.
Card components use a frosted-glass / glassmorphism surface style with backdrop-blur.
Stress gauge and breathing circle have a soft ambient glow effect.
Page transitions include a fade-in + slide-up entrance animation.
All interactive elements (buttons, tiles, toggles) have visible hover and active press states.
New accent color tokens (teal, sage, gold) are defined as OKLCH CSS variables in `frontend/src/index.css`.
Visual consistency is maintained across light mode, dark mode, mobile, tablet, and desktop viewports.

constraints: Do not use Firebase, Cloud Functions, or any external backend services â€” all data storage and deletion logic must be implemented in the Motoko single-actor backend (`backend/main.mo`).
Do not edit any file under `frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.ts`, `frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts`, `frontend/src/main.tsx`, or `frontend/src/components/ui`.
All authorization checks in Motoko functions must verify `msg.caller` matches the data owner before any mutation.
Biometric re-authentication and BLE encryption are browser/device-level features; implement UI affordances and document limitations â€” do not simulate security that does not exist.
Do not introduce any external databases, cloud providers, or third-party auth services.
nonGoals: Firebase Firestore, Firebase Authentication, and Firebase Cloud Functions integration â€” the platform uses ICP/Motoko only.
Real-time BLE communication or actual hardware pairing â€” simulated sensor data is already in place.
Email delivery of exported data files â€” only in-app browser downloads are supported.
Native mobile app builds (iOS/Android APK) â€” this is a PWA.
Multi-factor authentication via SMS or TOTP â€” only Internet Identity is the supported auth mechanism.
Actual AES-256 client-side encryption of stored data beyond what ICP provides natively.

required: Abstract calming gradient background with soft teal, deep indigo, and sage green organic blobs on a dark navy base, no text (privacy-dashboard-bg.dim_1200x800.png)
TRANQUIL app logo mark â€” a minimal outlined lotus or wave symbol in soft teal on transparent background (tranquil-logo-mark.dim_256x256.png)
